a day; the sheet metal workers have
had their scale increased 40 per cent
and the cooks and kitchen helpers
have received the same advance.
These three unions have also been
allowed corresponding shortening
of hours.


The bookbinders installed the fol-
lowing officers at their last regular
meeting: E. p. Walker, president; V.
]. Langville, vice president; William
Dye, corresponding and recording sec
rotary; INLAND Flint, financial secre-
tary; Martin Brunk. treasurer; E F.
Ringer, sergeant at arms. INLAND
Flint, Martin Brunk and William
Dye were appointed delegates to the
trades council.


A committee from the Retail
Clerks union called on the Retail
Grocers' association last night. An
agreement was made to enforce the
closing of stores at o'clock during
November, December, january and
February. The stores will be kept
open on Saturday until o'clock. Dur
ing the spring and summer months
the stores will remain open on Sat-
urday until o'clock.
